All data reflects typical UK market ranges for specialist rebuilt units.

cards.altText: Chrysler Crossfire M112.975 engine - petrol 3.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain guide deterioration - plastic guides wear out, causing cold-start rattles.
Oil cooler seal leaks - seals degrade from heat, causing oil to pool near the filter housing.

cards.altText: Chrysler Crossfire M112.976 engine - petrol 3.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: Supercharger coupler snapping - rubber isolator wears out, causing loud whining and boost loss.
Timing chain guide deterioration - shared vulnerability with the naturally aspirated variant.

cards.altText: Chrysler Crossfire M112.975 engine - petrol 3.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: Camshaft position sensor failure - causes intermittent stalling or no-start conditions on aging units.
Oil cooler seal seepage - particularly prevalent on higher-mileage early examples.

commonProblems.h2: Chrysler Crossfire Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Chrysler Crossfire's reliability is heavily tied to its Mercedes-Benz M112 V6 engine. While fundamentally robust, it is prone to specific issues like timing chain guide wear, oil cooler seal leaks, and (on SRT-6 models) supercharger coupler failure. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address these original failure points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Chrysler Crossfire Timing Chain Failure - Why M112 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler Crossfire 3.2 V6 Coupe, 3.2 V6 Roadster, SRT-6 (2004-2008, M112.975 & M112.976)
problems.rootCause: The original plastic timing chain guide rails become brittle and wear down over time, leading to chain slack, rattling on cold starts, and potential catastrophic valve damage if the chain jumps.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacing plastic guides with updated reinforced versions.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before chain stretch or valve damage occurs.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Installing a complete engine with updated guides, new chains, and tensioners. Includes 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: The definitive fix. Eliminates the risk of prior or future timing damage.

problems.recommendation: If you hear a distinct rattle on cold starts, act immediately. If the engine has already suffered interference damage, a simple chain replacement is insufficient. Opting for a full rebuilt engine ensures you get a reliable powertrain with the timing system flaw permanently resolved.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M112 engine prices for your Chrysler Crossfire

problems.group: Chrysler Crossfire Oil Cooler Seal Leak
problems.h4: Chrysler Crossfire Oil Cooler Seal Leak - Why V6 Engines Seep Oil
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler Crossfire 3.2 V6, SRT-6 (2004-2008, M112.975 & M112.976)
problems.rootCause: The oil cooler seals, located near the oil filter housing, degrade due to heat cycles, causing oil to pool around the bell housing or under the car.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removing the oil cooler and fitting new high-temperature resistant seals.
repairOptions.longevity: A good fix if the engine block and cooler are undamaged, but other age-related leaks may follow.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Installing a fully reconditioned engine with all-new seals, gaskets, and a refreshed oiling system. Includes 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage engines where multiple leaks and internal wear are present.

problems.recommendation: On a Crossfire with over 80,000 miles, chasing individual oil leaks can become a costly game of whack-a-mole. A reconditioned engine resolves all sealing issues at once and restores internal clearances, offering better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for M112.975/M112.976 engine replacement

problems.group: Chrysler Crossfire SRT-6 Supercharger Coupler Failure
problems.h4: Chrysler Crossfire SRT-6 Supercharger Coupler Failure - Why Boost is Lost
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler Crossfire SRT-6 Coupe, SRT-6 Roadster (2005-2008, M112.976)
problems.rootCause: The rubber isolator coupler inside the Eaton supercharger snout wears out and snaps, causing a loud metallic whining noise and an immediate loss of supercharger boost.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removing the supercharger snout and installing an updated polyurethane coupler.
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if the supercharger internals are otherwise healthy and no belt damage occurred.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Installing a reconditioned SRT-6 engine with a fully serviced supercharger, new coupler, and verified boost pressure. Includes 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: The safest route if the snapped coupler caused secondary damage to the supercharger or engine.

problems.recommendation: While a coupler replacement is a known fix, it requires removing the supercharger. If your SRT-6 has high mileage, combining this with a full engine recondition ensures the entire powertrain is refreshed and ready for high-performance use.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt SRT-6 M112.976 engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Chrysler Crossfire
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're battling timing chain rattle in your 3.2 V6, oil leaks, or supercharger issues in your SRT-6, EngineMarket makes it simple to find a lasting solution. Our network of trusted UK Chrysler Crossfire specialists provides rebuilt and reconditioned engines with upgraded components to fix the original design flaws, all backed by a 12-month warranty and available with nationwide supply & fit. Compare prices today and get back on the road without paying the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Chrysler Crossfire Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Chrysler Crossfire and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

faq.h2: Chrysler Crossfire Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Chrysler Crossfire engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A complete Chrysler Crossfire engine replacement typically costs between £1,200 and £3,500 for a rebuilt or reconditioned unit, supply only. Adding supply and fit will increase this by around £500-£1,200.
keyPoints: Standard 3.2 V6 (M112.975) rebuilt units start from £1,200.
SRT-6 supercharged (M112.976) rebuilt units start from £2,000.
Prices vary based on the specific engine code and supplier.
Supply and fit packages offer a single, consolidated warranty.
items.cta: Compare Chrysler Crossfire engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: What is the most reliable replacement engine for a Chrysler Crossfire?
items.answer: The most reliable replacement is a professionally rebuilt M112.975 or M112.976 engine that features updated timing chain guides and new oil cooler seals to fix the original design flaws.
keyPoints: A rebuilt engine corrects known failure points with new parts.
The M112 platform benefits from widespread Mercedes parts availability.
Avoid cheap used engines for high-mileage vehicles with known issues.
Always choose a unit with a minimum 12-month warranty.
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Chrysler Crossfire engine with 12-month warranty

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Chrysler Crossfire?
items.answer: Yes, if the rest of the car (body, transmission, interior) is in good condition. A £1,500-£3,000 engine replacement is far more economical than scrapping the car or buying a new one.
keyPoints: The Chrysler Crossfire is a niche vehicle with low residual value.
Main dealers often quote prices that exceed the car's worth.
A specialist-supplied engine offers great value for a reliable classic.
It extends the life of a vehicle you are already familiar with.
items.cta: Get a free quote to see if engine replacement is right for you

items.question: Can I get a Chrysler Crossfire engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: Yes, absolutely. EngineMarket connects you with a network of RAC-approved UK garages who offer a complete supply and fit service with a single, consolidated warranty.
keyPoints: We handle sourcing the engine and booking the garage.
The engine is delivered directly to your local approved garage.
You get one invoice and one warranty covering parts and labour.
This removes the biggest barrier to customer-supplied engine installs.
items.cta: Book your Chrysler Crossfire engine supply and fit today

items.question: How do I find my Chrysler Crossfire engine code?
items.answer: Your engine code can be found on your V5C logbook, by checking the 8th digit of your VIN (9=3.2L NA, 8th digit varies for SRT-6), or stamped on the engine block near the oil filter housing.
keyPoints: The 8th VIN digit is the quickest way to identify your engine family.
Your V5C logbook will list the engine code under 'engine number'.
The physical stamp on the block is the most definitive method.
Providing the code ensures you get the exact correct replacement.
items.cta: Enter your reg to instantly find your Chrysler Crossfire engine code

items.question: What causes the timing chain rattle in a Chrysler Crossfire?
items.answer: The rattling noise is almost certainly from deteriorating plastic timing chain guide rails in the M112 V6, a common issue that can lead to chain jump and catastrophic valve damage if ignored.
keyPoints: The noise is often worse on cold start and may quieten as the engine warms.
It's a sign of internal wear, not just low oil level.
A top-end rebuild is risky; a full engine replacement is safer.
Replacement engines come with updated, reinforced guide rails.
items.cta: Get quotes for M112.975/M112.976 engine replacement

items.question: Why does my Chrysler Crossfire SRT-6 make a loud whining noise?
items.answer: The loud whining noise is almost certainly from a snapped supercharger coupler in the M112.976 engine, a known weakness that causes an immediate loss of boost and requires specialist attention.
keyPoints: The noise is accompanied by a noticeable loss of acceleration.
It's a specific failure point of the Eaton supercharger snout.
A coupler replacement is possible, but full engine refresh is safer.
Replacement SRT-6 engines come with updated polyurethane couplers.
items.cta: Compare rebuilt SRT-6 M112.976 engine prices

items.question: Are Chrysler Crossfire 3.2 V6 engines reliable?
items.answer: The core M112 3.2L V6 is fundamentally a robust and durable Mercedes-Benz design, but it is plagued by age-related plastic timing guide wear and oil cooler seal leaks that require proactive management.
keyPoints: The engine itself is a proven, durable design from Mercedes.
Seal and guide issues are the primary cause of failure.
Fixing these root causes via a rebuild is key to long-term reliability.
They are cost-effective to replace due to parts commonality.
items.cta: Compare rebuilt M112 engine prices for your Chrysler Crossfire

items.question: Will a 2006 Chrysler Crossfire engine fit a 2004 model?
items.answer: Yes, the M112.975 engine remained largely unchanged throughout the 2004-2008 production run, making cross-year compatibility highly straightforward for the naturally aspirated variants.
keyPoints: The 3.2L block and ancillary components are consistent across years.
SRT-6 (M112.976) engines are only compatible with other SRT-6 models.
Always match the engine to your exact variant (NA vs Supercharged).
Our system ensures you only see engines compatible with your car.
